skill.name: backend-ralph-plan
skill.description: Create structured plan directories for backend Django work with Ralph loop prompts, task files, quality gates, and verification steps.
skill.summary: Builds backend Django implementation plans for iterative Ralph loop execution.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command-like strings, weak-crypto terms, and obfuscation patterns. Review found no confirmed malicious payload or prompt-injection attempt, but the skill intentionally uses Bash, writes plan files, and instructs agents to run project commands and git commits.

medium_findings.title: Bash Workflow Executes Project Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash and creates prompts that run lint, format, type check, Django, pytest, grep, and pre-commit commands. This is legitimate for backend planning, but commands and paths should come only from trusted project configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution pattern is directly present in the skill metadata and prompt templates. The context shows quality-gate automation rather than malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Broad Git Staging May Capture Unrelated Files
medium_findings.description: The generated Ralph prompt stages all changes before committing. In a dirty repository, this can include unrelated local edits or sensitive files that were not part of the planned task.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt explicitly uses git add -A before commit. The risk depends on repository state, but the operational behavior is clear.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static alerts appear in markdown headings, placeholders, tables, and example text. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code, encoded payloads, or obfuscated executable logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation or templates, not executable cryptographic or obfuscated code. No encoded payload or hash algorithm implementation was present in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill generates markdown prompts that instruct an agent to execute shell commands for validation and git workflow steps. This should be disclosed because command values can vary by project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and repeated in the templates. The commands are standard development checks, so the concern is capability and misuse rather than confirmed malware.

content.user_title: Plan Backend Django Work With Ralph
content.value_statement: Backend Django work can fail when tasks, tests, and quality gates are unclear. This skill creates a structured plan for iterative Ralph execution.
actual_capabilities: Creates a PLAN.md structure with tasks, dependencies, progress tracking, and quality gates.
Creates a RALPH-PROMPT.md template for iterative Ralph loop execution.
Breaks backend Django work into ordered task files with scope, tests, and completion criteria.
Defines lint, type check, Django, test, coverage, and shortcut detection commands.
Provides a complete user preferences example with model, service, API, and caching tasks.
limitations: Designed for backend Django projects, not frontend or general product planning.
Requires trusted command inputs because generated prompts may run shell commands.
Does not implement application code by itself; it creates execution plans and prompts.
Works best when task scope, paths, and test filters are known before planning.

use_cases.title: Plan a Django Feature
use_cases.description: Create ordered backend tasks with tests, dependencies, and completion gates before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Prepare Autonomous Iteration
use_cases.description: Generate a Ralph prompt that tells an agent how to resume, verify, commit, and continue work.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Document required lint, type, Django, test, coverage, and regression checks for every task.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one backend feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backend Ralph plan for a Django feature named [feature]. Use app path [path] and test filter [filter].

prompt_templates.title: Split a Feature Into Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert a feature brief into ordered work
prompt_templates.prompt: Break this Django backend feature into Ralph-ready tasks with dependencies, tests, and quality gates: [feature brief].

prompt_templates.title: Customize Tooling Commands
prompt_templates.scenario: Adapt the plan to project-specific wrappers
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backend Ralph plan using lint [command], type check [command], Django [command], and pytest [command].

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Full Execution Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create PLAN.md, task files, and RALPH-PROMPT.md for [feature]. Include dependencies, blockers, coverage targets, and final validation.

output_examples.input: A user preferences API with model, service, API endpoints, and caching.
output: A four-task execution plan ordered by dependency.
A Ralph prompt that resumes work, runs quality gates, commits progress, and checks completion.
Task files with scope, tests, completion criteria, and notes.

output_examples.input: A Django backend feature with custom lint, type check, and pytest commands.
output: A plan directory structure with project-specific command names.
Quality gate tables for lint, types, tests, coverage, and Django checks.
Final validation instructions for all tasks.

best_practices: Use trusted command values and paths when generating plans.
Keep each task small enough to complete with one focused verification cycle.
Review generated git and test commands before running Ralph loop execution.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for quick prototypes that do not need quality gates.
Do not provide command strings from untrusted users or issue trackers.
Do not rely on broad git staging when the repository has unrelated changes.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a plan directory with PLAN.md, task files, and RALPH-PROMPT.md for backend Django work.

faq.question: Does it write application code?
faq.answer: No. It creates planning and execution prompt files that guide a later implementation loop.

faq.question: Which projects fit best?
faq.answer: It fits Django backend features with clear paths, tests, dependencies, and quality requirements.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally uses Bash workflows and generated prompts that can execute project commands.

faq.question: Can I customize commands?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports project-specific lint, format, type check, Django, and test commands.

faq.question: What should I review before running it?
faq.answer: Review generated shell commands, file paths, test filters, and git staging instructions.
